edges.description: Loss of terminal phalangeal bone produces radiographic acro-osteolysis.

nodes.description: Localized osteolysis affects the distal phalanges of the hands and/or feet. In idiopathic cases, this bone resorption remains unexplained after evaluation for secondary and syndromic causes.

evidence.reference_title: Acro-osteolysis: imaging, differential diagnosis, and disposition review.
evidence.snippet: Acro-osteolysis is the osseous destruction of the hand or foot distal phalanges.
Both cases represent primary idiopathic acro-osteolysis, as the bony changes are limited to the distal phalanges of the hands and feet.
